message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run param to Image.save() - needs to touch Image.py save() method and its test file. Let me know your feature's files so we can avoid overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My feature: add backup=True/backup_suffix params to Image.save() to backup existing files before overwrite. I only need to touch Image.py (save method) and its tests. Please propose your files.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need save() in the same two files, so file-level disjointness isn't possible. Propose sequential handoff instead: I'll implement dry_run now (small, localized change near the top of save() logic), send DONE with the diff summary, then you rebase your backup/backup_suffix changes on top of my version and send your own DONE. This avoids simultaneous writes clobbering each other. OK with me going first?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both touch save() in Image.py and test_image.py, so full file disjointness isn't possible. Let's split by region instead: I'll only add a new 'backup'/'backup_suffix' kwarg to save()'s signature (appended after existing params), insert a small self-contained backup step near the top of save() body (before the file is opened for writing), and append new test functions (test_save_backup*) at the END of test_image.py. I will not touch your dry_run code/signature/docstring lines. Please do the same for dry_run: append your param after mine in the signature, keep your logic in its own block, and append your tests at the end too. Ok?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed - sequential handoff. You go first with dry_run, send DONE when finished, then I'll implement backup/backup_suffix on top of your changes and send my own DONE. Will wait for your DONE before editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. I will append dry_run param to save()'s signature after your backup/backup_suffix params, keep my dry_run logic in its own self-contained block, won't touch your backup code/lines, and will append my test_save_dry_run* tests at the END of test_image.py after your new tests. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: dry_run implementation complete and tested (4 new tests passing, full test_image.py suite passing 196/197 with 1 pre-existing skip). Summary of my changes: added 'dry_run: bool' param popped from **params in save() (line ~2552), added docstring entry, and in the file-open block redirect to an in-memory io.BytesIO() when dry_run is True instead of opening/creating the real file, guarding the close()/cleanup calls with 'and not dry_run'. My changes are localized to lines 2508-2513 (docstring), 2551-2552 (pop), and 2567-2601 (open/write/close logic) in Image.py, plus 4 new test methods appended at the end of TestImage in test_image.py (right before 'class TestImageBytes'). You're clear to implement backup/backup_suffix on top of this now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: backup/backup_suffix implementation complete and tested (4 new tests passing, full test_image.py suite 196 passed/1 skipped). Changes: popped 'backup' (bool, default False) and 'backup_suffix' (str, default '.bak') from params before they hit encoderinfo; added docstring entries; in the open_fp block, before opening the file for writing, if backup=True and the target file already exists, copy it via shutil.copy2 to filename+backup_suffix. Localized to: import shutil (top), docstring block ~2506-2513, param pop ~2554-2555, and a small if-block ~2573-2578 right at the top of 'if open_fp:'. Did not touch your dry_run lines. Wrote final patch.txt with git diff (only Image.py + test_image.py, my changes only, since it looks like we're each in isolated working copies).
